back_translation: In the beginning the Word existed, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
This One was with God in the beginning.
All things were created through Him, and there is nothing that exists which was not created through Him.
In Him there was life, and that life was the light of all humanity.
That light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome that light.
There was a man whom God sent, named John.
This man came to be a witness, in order to bear witness to that light, so that everyone might believe through him.
He himself was not that light, but he came to bear witness to that light.
The true light, which gives light to everyone, was coming into the world.
He was in the world, and the world was created through Him, but the world did not know Him.
He came to His own things, but His own people did not receive Him.
But all who received Him, that is, those who believe in His name, He gave the right to be children of God.
These were not born of bloodlines, nor of the desire of the flesh, nor of the will of a man, but were born of God.
And the Word took on the state of becoming a human being and came to dwell among us, and we all saw His glory, the glory befitting the only Son from the Father, full of grace and truth.
John bore witness about Him and proclaimed loudly, saying, 'This is the One I spoke about, the One who comes after me is above me, because He existed before me.'
Because we all received from His fullness, that is, grace upon grace.
Because the Law was given through Moses, but grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
No one has ever seen God at any time. The only God who is intimately in the Father's bosom — that One has revealed [the Father] to us.
This is the testimony of John, when the Jewish leaders from Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, 'Who are you?'
He acknowledged and did not deny; he acknowledged clearly, 'I am not the Christ.'
So they asked him, 'Then who are you? Are you Elijah?' He answered, 'I am not.' 'Are you that Prophet?' He answered, 'No.'
So they said to him, 'Who are you, so that we may bring an answer to those who sent us? What do you say about yourself?'
He said, 'I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, “Make straight the way of the Lord,” as Isaiah the prophet said.'
Now those who had been sent came from the Pharisees.
They asked him, 'If you are not the Christ, not Elijah, nor that Prophet, why then do you baptize?'
John answered them, 'I baptize with water, but there is one standing among you whom you do not know.
He is the One who comes after me; even merely the strap of His sandal I am not worthy to untie.'
These events took place at the village of Bethany on the east side of the Jordan River, where John was baptizing.
The next day, John saw Jesus coming toward him, and he said, 'Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world.'
This is the One I spoke about: 'A man who comes after me is above me, because He existed before me.'
I myself did not know Him, but in order that He might be revealed to Israel, I came baptizing with water.
John testified further, 'I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and resting on Him.

I myself did not know Him, but the One who sent me to baptize with water, He said to me, “The one on whom you see the Spirit descending and resting, that is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.”
And I have seen, and have testified, that this is the One whom God has chosen.'
The next day, John was standing again, and two of his disciples were with him.
When he saw Jesus walking by, John said, 'Behold, the Lamb of God!'
When the two disciples heard John speak in this way, they followed Jesus.
Jesus turned His face, looked and saw them following, so He asked them, 'What do you want?' They said to Him, 'Rabbi (which means “Teacher”), where are You staying?'
He said to them, 'Come and see.' So they went and saw where He was staying, and stayed with Him that day. It was about four o'clock in the afternoon.
Andrew, the younger brother of Simon Peter, was one of the two who heard John and followed Jesus.
Andrew first found his brother Simon, and said to him, 'We have found the Messiah!' (which means 'the Christ').
Andrew brought Simon to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, 'You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas' (which means 'Peter').
The next day, Jesus desired to go to the region of Galilee. He found Philip, and said to him, 'Follow Me.'
Philip was from the city of Bethsaida, which was the city of Andrew and Peter.
Philip found Nathanael and said to him, 'We have found the One whom Moses wrote about in the Law and whom the prophets spoke about, that is, Jesus of Nazareth, son of Joseph.'
Nathanael said to him, 'Can anything good come from the city of Nazareth?' Philip answered, 'Come and see.'
Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward Him, and said about him, 'Behold, a true Israelite, in whom there is no deceit.'
Nathanael asked Him, 'How do You know me?' Jesus answered him, 'Before Philip called you, while you were sitting under the fig tree, I saw you there.'
Nathanael answered Him, 'Rabbi, You are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!'
Jesus answered him, 'You believe because I told you I saw you under the fig tree, do you? You will see things greater than this.'
And He said to him, 'Truly, truly I say to you all, you all will see heaven opened, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man.'
